nodes.name: DedispersionKernel
nodes.description: Applies a dedispersion kernel to input data using a pre-computed delay table, transforming the data across different dispersion measure (DM) steps.

inputs.name: input_data
inputs.constraints: Raw observational data array.

inputs.name: delay_table
inputs.constraints: Table of delays to apply for each channel and DM step.

inputs.name: dm_steps
inputs.constraints: Number of dispersion measures to process.

inputs.name: time_downsample
inputs.constraints: Factor by which to downsample the time series.

inputs.name: down_ndata
inputs.constraints: The size of the output data array after downsampling.

inputs.name: nchans
inputs.constraints: Number of frequency channels in the input data.

inputs.name: shared_mem_size
inputs.constraints: Size of shared memory for the computational kernel (e.g., on a GPU).

inputs.name: block_dim_x
inputs.constraints: The block dimension in the x-axis for the computational kernel.

nodes.type_signature: (input_data: numpy.ndarray, delay_table: numpy.ndarray, dm_steps: int, time_downsample: int, down_ndata: int, nchans: int, shared_mem_size: int, block_dim_x: int) -> numpy.ndarray
nodes.conceptual_summary: ParameterizedShearAndProject. Transforms a 2D tensor (representing parallel sequences) by applying a series of parameterized, non-uniform shearing operations along one axis, followed by a projection (summation) along the other. For each parameter in a discrete set, it generates a 1D vector by shifting each sequence according to a pre-computed lookup table and then summing the values across the sequences at each time step. The resulting 1D vectors are collected into a new 2D tensor, which may be subsequently downsampled along the sequence axis.. Applications: Seismology: Correcting for seismic wave dispersion in sensor array data, where different geological layers cause frequency-dependent delays, to align signals from multiple seismometers to detect a common event signature., Medical Imaging: Aligning frequency-dependent echoes in ultrasound imaging. As waves pass through different tissue types, this transform can 'refocus' returned signals from a transducer array to improve image clarity., Sonar/Hydroacoustics: Compensating for frequency-dependent propagation delays in underwater acoustic signals caused by temperature or salinity gradients, allowing a sensor array to align signals from a distant source to improve detection., Genomics: Aligning multiple sequence reads that have undergone systematic, position-dependent insertions or deletions, by testing a range of 'skew' parameters to find an optimal consensus sequence.

nodes.name: calculate_tile_channel_height
nodes.description: Calculates the number of frequency channels to process in a single tile, based on the simulated shared memory size and the width of the time block.

inputs.name: shared_mem_size
inputs.constraints: Total size of the simulated shared memory.

inputs.name: block_dim_x
inputs.constraints: Number of time samples in a block (tile width).

inputs.name: total_channels
inputs.constraints: Total number of frequency channels (Dch).

outputs.name: tile_channel_height
outputs.constraints: The number of channels per tile (Nch).

nodes.type_signature: (shared_mem_size: int, block_dim_x: int, total_channels: int) -> int

nodes.name: load_shifted_data_tile
nodes.description: For a specific dispersion measure, time block, and channel chunk, this atom loads a tile of data from the input, applying the appropriate time delays for each frequency channel.

inputs.name: input_data
inputs.constraints: Full input data array (channels x time).

inputs.name: delay_row
inputs.constraints: 1D array of time delays for the current dispersion measure.

inputs.name: time_downsample
inputs.constraints: Factor by which time samples are downsampled.

inputs.name: total_downsampled_time
inputs.constraints: Total number of time samples after downsampling (down_ndata).

inputs.name: total_channels
inputs.constraints: Total number of frequency channels (Dch).

inputs.name: tile_start_channel
inputs.constraints: The starting channel index for this tile (c).

inputs.name: tile_channel_height
inputs.constraints: The number of channels to load into this tile (niter).

inputs.name: tile_start_time
inputs.constraints: The starting time index for this block (Tini).

inputs.name: tile_time_width
inputs.constraints: The number of time samples in this block (block_dim_x).

outputs.name: shifted_data_tile
outputs.constraints: A 2D array (tile_channel_height x tile_time_width) with time-shifted data.

nodes.type_signature: (input_data: numpy.ndarray, delay_row: numpy.ndarray, time_downsample: int, total_downsampled_time: int, total_channels: int, tile_start_channel: int, tile_channel_height: int, tile_start_time: int, tile_time_width: int) -> numpy.ndarray

nodes.name: sum_tile_over_channels
nodes.description: Reduces a tile of time-shifted data by summing across the frequency channel axis, producing a partial sum for each time step within the block.

inputs.name: shifted_data_tile
inputs.constraints: The 2D array of time-shifted data from the loading step.

outputs.name: time_block_partial_sums
outputs.constraints: A 1D array of size tile_time_width containing the sum for each time step in the block.
